statement: Characterize the regime (microbatch count m*, width w*, or workload class) in which the Hybrid Zeckendorf P-stack via FactoredRationalLazyAccumulator achieves CI95 lower bound > 1.0 over GMP-Fraction on the exact-rational KAN substrate. Split out from rational_kan_hz_pstack_in_loop_20260420 on 2026-04-20.

n_sr_root.content: Prove: there exists a regime (m*, w*, or workload class) in which HZ P-stack via FactoredRationalLazyAccumulator achieves CI95 lower > 1.0 over GMP-Fraction on the exact-rational KAN substrate, OR prove no such regime exists and scope HZ advantage strictly to T1.5 dictionary workload.
n_sr_root.goal_before: Characterize the HZ-speedup regime for exact-rational KAN via FactoredRationalLazyAccumulator.

n_sr_inherit.content: Inherit evidence floor from the split: microbatch sweep m in {1..128}, width sweep w in {10..500}, bridge overhead breakdown (99.28 percent in Rust HZ primitive), inference-lane CI95 [0.77436, 1.07393], update-lane CI95 [0.07012, 0.07241], T1.5 dictionary 44.17x at 10M features.
n_sr_inherit.goal_before: Characterize the HZ-speedup regime for exact-rational KAN via FactoredRationalLazyAccumulator.
n_sr_inherit.outcome_reason: Evidence already on disk under artifacts/rational_kan_hz/paper_run/pstack_in_loop/ and artifacts/rational_kan_hz/paper_run/scaling/. No rerun needed to start this conjecture.

n_sr_extend_microbatch.content: Extend microbatch sweep beyond m=128 and attempt to locate the crossover empirically. Fit the observed monotone trend and test whether extrapolation is feasible within realistic training budgets.
n_sr_extend_microbatch.goal_before: Characterize the HZ-speedup regime for exact-rational KAN via FactoredRationalLazyAccumulator.

n_sr_algorithm_change.content: Algorithmic change: emit operand groups without LCM pre-collapse entirely (pure-integer per-denominator HZ accumulate), re-measure whether the Rust HZ primitive hot path reshapes and whether a smaller network-scale crossover emerges.
n_sr_algorithm_change.goal_before: Characterize the HZ-speedup regime for exact-rational KAN via FactoredRationalLazyAccumulator.

n_sr_prove_no_crossover.content: Prove (via bridge_overhead_breakdown + width_sweep + microbatch_sweep monotonicity + T1.5 contrast) that no realistic rational-KAN regime crosses, and formally scope HZ advantage to the T1.5 dictionary workload (small-magnitude operands, feature counts >> 1e4).
n_sr_prove_no_crossover.goal_before: Characterize the HZ-speedup regime for exact-rational KAN via FactoredRationalLazyAccumulator.

unexplored_strategies: emit operand groups without LCM pre-collapse
prove no crossover and scope to T1.5
key_blockers: 99.28 percent of P-stack time is inside the Rust HZ primitive - FFI migration cannot move the gate
promising_paths: algorithmic change to HZ decomposition (no LCM pre-collapse)
dead_ends: bridge IO engineering (JSON->FFI) - bounded above at 0.72 percent improvement
